title: Use of Metadata-Driven Approaches for Data Harmonization in the Medical Domain: Scoping Review
abstract: Background: Multisite clinical studies are increasingly using real-world data to gain real-world evidence. However, due to the heterogeneity of source data, it is difficult to analyze such data in a unified way across clinics. Therefore, the implementation of Extract-Transform-Load (ETL) or Extract-Load-Transform (ELT) processes for harmonizing local health data is necessary, in order to guarantee the data quality for research. However, the development of such processes is time-consuming and unsustainable. A promising way to ease this is the generalization of ETL/ELT processes. Objective: In this work, we investigate existing possibilities for the development of generic ETL/ELT processes. Particularly, we focus on approaches with low development complexity by using descriptive metadata and structural metadata. Methods: We conducted a literature review following the PRISMA (Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses) guidelines. We used 4 publication databases (ie, PubMed, IEEE Explore, Web of Science, and Biomed Center) to search for relevant publications from 2012 to 2022. The PRISMA flow was then visualized using an R-based tool (Evidence Synthesis Hackathon). All relevant contents of the publications were extracted into a spreadsheet for further analysis and visualization. Results: Regarding the PRISMA guidelines, we included 33 publications in this literature review. All included publications were categorized into 7 different focus groups (ie, medicine, data warehouse, big data, industry, geoinformatics, archaeology, and military). Based on the extracted data, ontology-based and rule-based approaches were the 2 most used approaches in different thematic categories. Different approaches and tools were chosen to achieve different purposes within the use cases. Conclusions: Our literature review shows that using metadata-driven (MDD) approaches to develop an ETL/ELT process can serve different purposes in different thematic categories. The results show that it is promising to implement an ETL/ELT process by applying MDD approach to automate the data transformation from Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources to Observational Medical Outcomes Partnership Common Data Model. However, the determining of an appropriate MDD approach and tool to implement such an ETL/ELT process remains a challenge. This is due to the lack of comprehensive insight into the characterizations of the MDD approaches presented in this study. Therefore, our next step is to evaluate the MDD approaches presented in this study and to determine the most appropriate MDD approaches and the way to integrate them into the ETL/ELT process. This could verify the ability of using MDD approaches to generalize the ETL process for harmonizing medical data. © Yuan Peng, Franziska Bathelt, Richard Gebler, Robert Gött, Andreas Heidenreich, Elisa Henke, Dennis Kadioglu, Stephan Lorenz, Abishaa Vengadeswaran, Martin Sedlmayr.
authors: Peng, Y.; Bathelt, F.; Gebler, R.; Gött, R.; Heidenreich, A.; Henke, E.; Kadioglu, D.; Lorenz, S.; Vengadeswaran, A.; Sedlmayr, M.
source: JMIR Medical Informatics
keywords: data harmonization; ELT; ETL; Extract-Load-Transform; Extract-Transform-Load; interoperability; medical domain; metadata-driven

title: A review of recommendation systems based on the fusion of new technologies
abstract: The huge amount of data has grown exponentially with the development of Internet technology, and we have thus entered the era of information explosion. It is very difficult to get the target information that users are interested in or need in the huge amount of data. To solve the problem of information overload, recommendation systems play an important role. At present, recommendation systems have become a popular research area and have received a lot of attention with many application areas. However, recommendation systems suffer from the problems of cold start, data sparsity and poor interpretability, and many researches have been conducted to alleviate these problems. In this paper, we first give a brief overview of recommendation systems and traditional recommendation algorithms, then summarize the improvements made by recommendation systems with the introduction of auxiliary information, and finally summarize the challenges faced by recommendation systems and give the future development hotspots. © 2023 IEEE.
authors: Gao, M.; Yu, W.
source: ICIIBMS 2023 - 8th International Conference on Intelligent Informatics and Biomedical Sciences
keywords: collaborative filtering; federated learning; graph neural networks; knowledge graph; recommendation system

title: Integrating RAG and LLM for Automated Code Review in Practice
abstract: With the rapid development of the digital economy, software development capability has become a key element of corporate core competitiveness. When facing urgent projects or short-term manpower shortages, companies often resort to temporary outsourcing solutions, leading to significant code quality issues in outsourced projects. Traditional static code analysis tools suffer from limitations such as delayed rule updates, insufficient cross-module analysis capabilities, and weak interpretability of results, making them inadequate to meet current demands. Advances in large language models (LLM) and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) offer new possibilities for code quality management. However, current LLM-based solutions still face challenges including insufficient comment accuracy, low practicality, and a lack of targeted systematic improvement mechanisms. To address these issues, this study constructs a code graph knowledge base to provide effective contextual information and proposes a novel AI Code Review (AI-CR) framework. Experimental results demonstrate that the framework significantly improves adoption rate and developer trust. In comparative experiments, the adoption rate increased by 18.5%, surpassing that of contemporaneous manual reviews.
authors: L. Yang; Y. Chen
source: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Information Processing Technology (ISCIPT)
keywords: Code Review;Large Language Model;Retrieval-Augmented Generation

title: Research on Tourist Review Sentiment Classification Algorithm with Integrated Attention Mechanism
abstract: Addressing the challenges in tourist review sentiment analysis, including the complexity of comment sequences, difficulty in capturing contextual semantics, and the intricacy of identifying domain-specific vocabulary, this paper proposes a novel model that integrates an attention mechanism with Whole Word Masking BERT (WWM-BERT) and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM). The proposed model leverages WWM-BERT's word-level masking strategy to optimize semantic extraction in comments, effectively capturing specific tourism terminology. Meanwhile, the integration of an attention mechanism with Bi-LSTM enhances the understanding of long- and short-term dependencies within the sequence of tourist reviews, thereby refining the model's sentiment comprehension and accuracy. Experimentation, based on crawled tourist user comment data, is conducted and compared with other models. Results indicate that our model achieves an impressive accuracy rate of 92.5% in identifying user sentiment on the test set, outperforming alternative models.
authors: Duan, Ganglong and Bai, Lihao and Shi, Wenlong
source: Proceedings of the International Conference on Machine Learning, Pattern Recognition and Automation Engineering

title: Deep Learning in Physical Layer: Review on Data Driven End-to-End Communication Systems and Their Enabling Semantic Applications
abstract: Deep learning (DL) has revolutionized wireless communication systems by introducing data-driven end-to-end (E2E) learning, where the physical layer (PHY) is transformed into DL architectures to achieve peak optimization. Leveraging DL for E2E optimization in PHY significantly enhances its adaptability and performance in complex wireless environments, meeting the demands of advanced network systems such as 5G and beyond. Furthermore, this evolution of data-driven PHY optimization has also enabled advanced semantic applications across various modalities, including text, image, audio, video, and multimodal transmissions. These applications elevate communication from bit-level to semantic-level intelligence, making it capable of discerning context and intent. Although the PHY, as a DL architecture, plays a crucial role in enabling semantic communication (SemCom) systems, comprehensive studies that integrate both E2E communication and SemCom systems remain significantly underexplored. This highlights the novelty and potential of these integrative fields, marking them as a promising research domain. Therefore, this article provides a comprehensive review of the emerging field of data-driven PHY for E2E communication systems, emphasizing their role in enabling semantic applications across various modalities. It also identifies key challenges and potential research directions, serving as a crucial guide for future advancements in DL for E2E communication and SemCom systems.
authors: N. Islam; S. Shin
source: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
keywords: 5G and beyond wireless communication systems;artificial intelligence;deep learning;end-to-end communication;end-to-end learning;goal-oriented communication;semantic communication;semantic distortion

title: Footprint of Semantics in ICT
abstract: In this paper we have been reviewing the incorporation of Semantics in various Information and Communication Technologies (ICT). Our extensive literature search resulted in a wide range of ICT applications in various fields. A set of requirements is necessary for the successful integration of semantics. The advantages and disadvantages of such an incorporation are pointed out, followed by the conclusions and a series of open issues that will absorb our future research efforts.

title: Internet of Things (IoT) in Smart Cities: A Statistical Survey
abstract: Internet of things is progressing very rapidly and involving multiple domains of everyday life including environment, governance, healthcare system, transportation system, energy management system, etc. smart city is a platform for collecting and storing the information that is accessed through various sensor-based IoT devices and make their information available in required and authorized domains. This interoperability can be achieved by semantic web technology. In this paper, I have reviewed multiple papers related to IoT in Smart Cities and presented a comparison among the semantic parameters. Moreover, I’ve presented my future domain of research which is about delivering the COVID-19 patients report to the concerned domains by the healthcare system domain.
authors: M. W. Iqbal; K. Khaliq; N. A. Al-Dmour; M. Aqeel; N. Ali; K. Hamid

title: A Review of Methods Using Large Language Models in News Recommendation Systems
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are widely used in natural language processing tasks due to their powerful semantic understanding and knowledge integration capabilities. Numerous existing recommendation studies consider recommendation tasks as a type of natural language processing, and thus LLMs have consequently brought new changes to the recommendation system paradigm. Existing research on recommendations using LLMs partly utilizes their rich data information, fine-grained user profiling, and expanded recommendation content to improve recommendation effectiveness. Additionally, some and partly studies directly uses LLMs to implement a generative recommendation paradigm. This paper adopts the literature review method to systematically sort out the current research status of news recommendation based on LLMs and classifies and summarizes the relevant research. To comprehensively understand the research in the field of news recommendation using LLMs, this paper introduces the current major work in the field of news recommendation from the two categories of generative LLM-assisted recommendation and direct generative recommendation and summarizes the current work as well as the potential future research directions and challenges.
authors: X. Li; S. Feng; X. Zhang
source: 2024 IEEE/ACIS 27th International Conference on Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Networking and Parallel/Distributed Computing (SNPD)
keywords: News Recommendation;Large Language Models;Generative News Recommendation
title_norm: a review of methods using large large language models in news recommender systems
abstract_norm: large large language models (large language models) are widely used in natural language processing tasks due to their powerful semantic understanding and knowledge integration capabilities. numerous existing recommendation studies consider recommendation tasks as a type of natural language processing, and thus large language models have consequently brought new changes to the recommender systems paradigm. existing research on recommendations using large language models partly utilizes their rich data information, fine-grained user profiling, and expanded recommendation content to improve recommendation effectiveness. additionally, some and partly studies directly uses large language models to implement a generative recommendation paradigm. this paper adopts the literature review method to systematically sort out the current research status of news recommendation based on large language models and classifies and summarizes the relevant research. to comprehensively understand the research in the field of news recommendation using large language models, this paper introduces the current major work in the field of news recommendation from the two categories of generative large language models-assisted recommendation and direct generative recommendation and summarizes the current work as well as the potential future research directions and challenges.
